raw_consumer_statement:
My sister passed away in XXXX, and I ordered flowers from Amex merchant XXXX. 
The flowers delivered where not described as on their website. Flowers also where delivered wilted and look to be old flowers. I reached out to Amex merchant XXXX to express my dissatisfaction. XXXX would not exchange or credit my card. I than reached out to my Amex to file a claim. The merchant stated that I never called them to complain but for some strange reason they had credited my Amex {$130.00} about one half of the charge. I find this strange if the merchant stated to Amex that I never contacted them why would they credit my card half of the charge. I have reopen this dispute with my Amex on 3 separate occasions and the reply I get from Amex is that they are federal regulated. I have a Amex XXXX card for over 40 years with Amex and can not believe I have to continue to reopen the dispute with them. 
I believe I have rights as a consumer but I believe Amex has not actually read the response from XXXX since XXXX stated I've not contacted them. Unfortinitly XXXX is a big merchant with Amex and Amex does not want to jeperdize that relationship. My sister passed away in XXXX, and I ordered flowers from XXXX, an Amex merchant. Unfortunately, the flowers that were delivered did not match the description on their website. Additionally, the flowers arrived wilted and appeared to be old. I reached out to XXXX to express my dissatisfaction, but they refused to exchange the flowers or issue a credit to my card. 

Subsequently, I contacted American Express to file a claim. The merchant claimed that I never called to complain, yet somehow they credited my Amex account {$130.00}, which is about half of the total charge. I find this situation perplexing. If XXXX stated to Amex that I never contacted them, why would they issue a partial credit? 

I have reopened this dispute with Amex on three separate occasions, but their response has been that they are federally regulated. I have held an American Express XXXX card for over 40 years and can not believe I have to keep reopening this issue. 

I believe I have rights as a consumer, but it feels as though Amex has not thoroughly reviewed XXXX 's response. XXXX is a large merchant with Amex, and I suspect that Amex does not want to jeopardize that relationship.
